1	# Archon Project
2	
3	You are either the plan agent, a prover agent, a subagent (one of the descriptors in `.archon/subagents/`), or the review agent. Read `PROGRESS.md` to determine your role and current objectives. Keep workspace tidy. Prefer existing MCP tools.
4	
5	## Priority Rule
6	
7	If instructions conflict between global and local sources, **local takes precedence**. Specifically:
8	- Prompts in `.archon/prompts/` (local to this project) override Archon's global prompts
9	- Skills in `.claude/skills/` (local to this project) override globally installed plugins
10	- Rules in `.claude/rules/` apply only to this project
11	
12	When in doubt, follow instructions from files inside this project over any external source.
13	
14	## Skills
15	- archon-lean4: installed as `lean4@archon-local` plugin (live-linked to Archon source) — provides `/archon-lean4:prove`, `/archon-lean4:golf`, `/archon-lean4:doctor`, and other Lean4 commands
16	
17	## Tools
18	
19	Project tools live in `.claude/tools/` as directly-executable scripts. List them with `ls .claude/tools/` and run `<path> --help` to learn each tool's purpose and usage. Invoke them via the Bash tool. Project MCP servers (Lean LSP, etc.) are registered in `.claude/settings.json` / `.mcp.json` and surface as `mcp__*` tools.
20	
21	Two always-present scripts:
22	
23	- **`archon-informal-agent.py`** — call external LLMs (OpenAI/Gemini/OpenRouter) for informal mathematical reasoning. Useful when you need a second opinion or a paper-style proof sketch the Lean side can then formalize.
24	- **`archon-subagent.py`** — the generic subagent dispatcher (see "Subagents" below). One wrapper handles every subagent.
25	
26	## Subagents
27	
28	Subagents are descriptor files at `.archon/subagents/<name>.md`. Each starts with YAML frontmatter (`name`, `description`, `write_domain`, `read_only`, `can_spawn`, `default_enabled`, optional `mandatory: [<phase>...]`) followed by the prompt body the spawned Claude session reads.
29	
30	**You do NOT need to discover subagents yourself.** The plan and review prompts have an auto-generated **Available subagents** section at the top of each invocation that lists every enabled descriptor with its description, write-domain hint, and any `[MANDATORY]` flags. When you decide to invoke a specific subagent, read `.archon/subagents/<name>.md` for its full prompt and directive shape.
31	
32	**Invoke any subagent with the generic wrapper** (Bash tool, foreground):
33	
34	```
35	python3 .claude/tools/archon-subagent.py \
36	  --name <subagent-name> \
37	  --slug <kebab-slug> \
38	  --directive-file <path-to-directive.md> \
39	  --write-domain '<glob>'        # repeat for multiple
40	```
41	
42	The wrapper exits 0 on success, prints a one-line status, and writes the subagent's report to `.archon/task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`. It shells out to `archon subagent <name>`, so executions stream through the Archon JSONL log like a phase agent. The dispatch semaphore bounds total concurrent subagent processes by `loop.max_parallel`; the wrapper resolves `--parent-slug` from `ARCHON_SUBAGENT_SLUG` automatically so deeper-nested children inherit the hierarchy.
43	
44	**Always dispatch synchronously** (not via background `Bash` with `run_in_background: true`). The wrapper returns when the subagent has finished and the report is on disk; only then write your session summary. Background dispatch leaves the parent's session permanently summarized as "running in background", which is wrong as soon as the subagent completes.
45	
46	**Mandatory subagents.** A descriptor whose frontmatter sets `mandatory: [plan]` MUST be dispatched at least once during the plan phase (similarly for `[review]`). The catalog tags them `[MANDATORY]`. A post-phase audit warns when a mandatory dispatch is missing.
47	
48	## Key Files & Permissions
49	
50	All state files are in `.archon/`:
51	
52	| File | Plan Agent | Prover Agent | Subagents | Review Agent | User |
53	|------|-----------|-------------|---------------|-------------|------|
54	| `.archon/PROGRESS.md` | read + write | **read only** | do not read | read only | read |
55	| `.archon/STRATEGY.md` | **read + write** | do not read | do not read | do not read | read |
56	| `.archon/USER_HINTS.md` | read (then clear) | do not read | do not read | do not read | write |
57	| `.archon/task_pending.md` | read + write | **read only** | do not read | read only | read |
58	| `.archon/task_done.md` | read + write | **read only** | do not read | read only | read |
59	| `.archon/task_results/<file>.md` | read (collect results) | write (own file only) | write (`<name>-<slug>.md`) | read only | read |
60	| `.archon/proof-journal/` | read | do not access | do not access | **write** | read |
61	| `.archon/PROJECT_STATUS.md` | read | do not access | do not access | **write** (Knowledge Base only — session log moved to iter/iter-NNN/review.md) | read |
62	| `.archon/iter/iter-NNN/plan.md` | **write** (this iter only) | do not access | do not access | read (last K iters as context) | read |
63	| `.archon/iter/iter-NNN/review.md` | read (last K iters as context) | do not access | do not access | **write** (this iter only) | read |
64	| `.archon/iter/iter-NNN/objectives.md` | optional write (per-attempt detail) | do not access | do not access | read | read |
65	| `archon-protected.yaml` | **read** | **read** | **read** (write-capable subagents may rename file path only) | read | **write** |
66	| `.lean` files | do not edit | write (own file only, frozen protected signatures) | per descriptor: write-capable subagents (e.g. refactor) may edit per their declared write-domain (protected decls may be moved but not renamed/re-signed); read-only subagents do not edit | do not edit | write (via comments) |
67	| `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex` | **write** (informal prose, `\lean{...}` hints, structure) | do not edit | do not edit | **write** (markers only: `\leanok`, `\mathlibok`, `% NOTE:`, `\lean{...}` corrections) | read |
68	| `Challenges/<Name>.lean` | do not edit | fill sorries (when assigned) | write-capable when the descriptor's write_domain covers it | do not edit | read |
69	| `analogies/<slug>.md` | read (when relevant) | do not access | write-capable when the descriptor's write_domain covers it | do not access | read |
70	
71	**`.archon/REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md` is reserved for the interactive `archon refactor draft` / `archon refactor run` flow, run by hand by the mathematician.** The autonomous loop never reads or writes that file. The plan agent invokes the refactor subagent (when a `refactor` descriptor is installed) via the generic `archon-subagent.py --name refactor` wrapper with a fresh tempfile under `.archon/logs/iter-NNN/`. This keeps each loop-driven refactor independently logged and avoids any chance of stale-directive reuse. Older state files (`STRATEGY.md`, `task_pending.md`, `PROGRESS.md`, etc.) may contain leftover references to the old REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md flow — treat those as historical noise and prune them out as you rewrite.
72	
73	## Protected Declarations
74	
75	`archon-protected.yaml` at the project root lists declarations whose **signatures are frozen** by the mathematician. Every agent must consult it before editing a `.lean` file. Rules:
76	
77	- **Plan / prover / review agents**: read-only on protected signatures. You may fill proof bodies, but not rename, re-type, or reorder arguments.
78	- **Refactor agent**: may *move* a protected declaration to a different file (keeping name + signature verbatim) and must then update the path key in `archon-protected.yaml`. Refactor agents may never rename, re-type, delete, or re-sign a protected declaration.
79	- The `archon-protected.yaml` file can only be edited by the user, no declaration can be added or removed by any agent. The only allowed modification is updating the file path of an existing protected declaration when its location was changed.
80	
81	## Blueprint Marker Vocabulary
82	
83	The blueprint uses two active markers.
84	
85	- `\leanok` — inside a statement block when the declaration is formalized with at least a `sorry`; inside a proof block when the proof is fully closed with no `sorry`.
86	- `\mathlibok` — inside a statement block when the declaration already exists in Mathlib and the Archon side is a re-export/alias; no Archon proof obligation remains.
87	- No marker — the block is unformalized. If a block fails to translate, leave it unmarked and annotate with a `% NOTE:` comment.
88	
89	**`\leanok` is managed deterministically by the `sync_leanok` phase** that runs between the prover and review phases each iteration. It walks every chapter, looks up each `\lean{...}` declaration, runs `sorry_analyzer` + `lake env lean`, and adds/removes `\leanok` accordingly. Agents must NOT add or remove `\leanok` themselves — let the script do it.
90	
91	`\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, and `% NOTE:` annotations remain the review agent's domain (they require semantic judgement). The plan agent writes informal prose and `\lean{...}` hints; provers never touch the blueprint.
92	
93	## User Interaction
94	
95	Users provide hints in two places:
96	
97	- **Strategic hints** → `.archon/USER_HINTS.md`. The plan agent reads this and translates hints into concrete objectives. Provers never read this file.
98	- **File-specific hints** → `/- USER: ... -/` comments directly in `.lean` files. The prover that owns that file sees them naturally.
99	
100	## Agent Roles
101	
102	### Plan Agent
103	- Read `.archon/prompts/plan.md` for your full instructions
104	- Read `.archon/USER_HINTS.md` — incorporate hints, then clear them after acting
105	- Read `.archon/task_results/` — collect prover and subagent results, then update `task_pending.md` and `task_done.md`
106	- Optionally invoke **subagents** (discoverable via `ls .archon/subagents/`) by running `.claude/tools/archon-subagent.py --name <name> ...` via Bash, **before** writing prover objectives. See `.archon/prompts/plan.md` § "Subagent delegation" for the canonical ordering and full invocation pattern.
107	- Archive any subagent report you receive to `.archon/logs/iter-NNN/<name>-<slug>-report.md` so the dashboard can render it.
108	- Write `.archon/PROGRESS.md` with objectives for the next prover round
109	- Write informal prose in `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex` (except for marker updates) and `\lean{...}` hints for the provers
110	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or fill sorries yourself
111	
112	### Prover Agent
113	- Read `.archon/PROGRESS.md` for your current objectives (read only — do not edit it)
114	- Read the stage-specific prompt from `.archon/prompts/`:
115	  - autoformalize → `.archon/prompts/prover-autoformalize.md`
116	  - prover → `.archon/prompts/prover-prover.md`
117	  - polish → `.archon/prompts/prover-polish.md`
118	- Write results to `.archon/task_results/<your_file>.md`
119	- Write only to the `.lean` file(s) you are assigned — **never edit another agent's file**
120	- Check for `/- USER: ... -/` comments in your `.lean` file for file-specific hints
121	- **Do NOT edit blueprint chapters.** Marker updates are the review agent's responsibility. Flag in your task result which declarations are ready for which marker.
122	
123	### Subagents
124	
125	Subagents are descriptor-driven and discovered at runtime — there is no fixed roster baked into this file. Each lives at `.archon/subagents/<name>.md` (YAML frontmatter + prompt body). To see what's available **this iteration**, run `ls .archon/subagents/` and read each descriptor's frontmatter for purpose, write-domain hint, and directive shape.
126	
127	Subagents are **not** part of the autonomous loop's standing phases. Phase agents (plan, review) invoke them on demand via the generic wrapper:
128	
129	```
130	python3 .claude/tools/archon-subagent.py --name <name> --slug <slug> --directive-file <path> --write-domain '<glob>'
131	```
132	
133	Each subagent reads only what its directive points at — never `PROGRESS.md`, `STRATEGY.md`, or other phase-agent state — and writes a self-contained report at `.archon/task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`. The dispatching agent reads the report when the wrapper returns and may then update `STRATEGY.md` / `PROGRESS.md` based on the findings.
134	
135	Some descriptors set `can_spawn: true`, meaning that subagent may itself dispatch children; the per-iter dispatch semaphore caps total concurrent processes by `loop.max_parallel` so deep trees stay bounded.
136	
137	### Review Agent
138	- Read `.archon/prompts/review.md` for your full instructions
139	- Read `.archon/proof-journal/current_session/attempts_raw.jsonl` for structured prover attempt data
140	- Write session journal to `.archon/proof-journal/sessions/session_N/` (summary.md, milestones.jsonl, recommendations.md)
141	- Update `.archon/PROJECT_STATUS.md` with overall progress
142	- Maintain the **semantic** blueprint markers — `\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, `% NOTE: ...` annotations, stale `\notready` removal — in `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex`. Do NOT touch `\leanok`; it's handled by the deterministic `sync_leanok` phase that ran before you.
143	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or modify PROGRESS.md
144	
145	### Loop infrastructure (no agent role)
146	
147	Two non-agent steps run automatically each iteration to reduce the burden on the agents above:
148	
149	- **Iter sidecar init**: at iter start, `.archon/iter/iter-NNN/` is created so the plan + review agents have a stable destination for their per-iter narrative (`plan.md`, `review.md`, optional `objectives.md`). Top-level files (STRATEGY.md, PROJECT_STATUS.md, task_*.md) stay bounded across iters because per-iter content lives in the sidecars.
150	- **`sync_leanok`**: runs between prover and review, deterministically updating `\leanok` markers based on actual sorry counts and compilation status. Replaces what used to be a multi-page review-agent task.
151	
152	`sync_leanok` writes an inner-git commit (`archon[NNN/marker-sync]`) so its output is auditable and revertable.

1	# Prover — Prover Stage
2	
3	You are the prover agent in the proving stage. Your job: fill `sorry` placeholders with complete proofs.
4	
5	## Workflow
6	
7	1. Read `PROGRESS.md` for your current objectives (read only — do not edit it)
8	2. Read `task_pending.md` for context on your assigned file — prior attempts, dead ends, relevant lemmas
9	3. Check your `.lean` file for `/- USER: ... -/` comments — these are file-specific hints from the user
10	4. Before writing Lean code, you **MUST** consult the relevant blueprint chapter. Blueprints contain mathematical proof sketches; your formal proof must align with them. When stuck, re-reading the blueprint is often the fastest path forward.
11	5. Replace `sorry` with Lean proofs, pushing as far as possible
12	6. **Always save partial progress in the code.** If you cannot fully prove a sorry, replace it with your best attempt — commented-out proof steps, helper lemmas, partial `by` blocks with remaining `sorry` at the stuck point. The file must still compile (use scoped `sorry` for the stuck parts), but your work must be visible in the code for the next agent to continue from. NEVER revert to the original bare `sorry` — that wastes all your work.
13	7. Write results to `task_results/<your_file>.md` — what you tried, what worked, what's stuck, next steps
14	
15	**Write permissions**: You may only write to your assigned `.lean` file(s) and your `task_results/<file>.md`. Do NOT edit `PROGRESS.md`, `task_pending.md`, `task_done.md`, or other agents' files.
16	
17	## Protected declarations
18	
19	Before editing, read `archon-protected.yaml` at the project root. Declarations listed for your file have **frozen signatures** — you may fill their proof bodies but must not rename them, change their types, reorder their arguments, or weaken their hypotheses. Only the mathematician is allowed to edit protected signatures. 
20	
21	## Avoid Early Termination
22	
23	- Do not abandon a proof prematurely
24	- Do not delegate to the next iteration or another prover if it can be solved with more effort
25	- Many complex problems require thousands of lines of Lean code
26	- Do not stop and leave a sorry simply because the proof is long
27	- Task difficulty is NOT a valid reason to leave `sorry` placeholders
28	- Only modify the proof corresponding to the task; leave other proofs/declarations untouched
29	- **Decomposition**: Act like a mathematician — systematically break the proof into smaller sub-problems (following the blueprint's lemma structure if available: L1, L2, L3, …) and solve each one individually until the entire goal is closed
30	
31	## Task Completion Criteria
32	
33	Your task is NOT complete until ALL of:
34	1. Every `sorry` has been replaced with a complete proof
35	2. Zero axioms introduced
36	3. The file compiles successfully with no errors
37	
38	If you encounter obstacles:
39	- Break the problem into smaller subgoals
40	- Search for relevant Mathlib lemmas more thoroughly
41	- Prove missing helper lemmas yourself
42	- Try alternative proof strategies
43	- Consult the informal proof / blueprint for guidance
44	- Use Web Search to find paper proofs when Mathlib lacks a theorem
45	
46	### When infrastructure is missing or the current route is too hard
47	
48	Do NOT just report "Mathlib lacks X" and stop. Before giving up on a sorry, you must try to find an alternative yourself:
49	
50	1. **Use the informal agent** (`.claude/tools/archon-informal-agent.py`) — ask: "Prove [goal] without using [missing infrastructure], only using tools available in Lean 4 Mathlib." Even an imperfect sketch is valuable.
51	2. **Try the alternative** — if the informal agent gives you a route, attempt to formalize it.
52	3. **If you still can't solve it**, save what you learned for the plan agent:
53	   - Write the informal agent's alternative proof sketch to `informal/<theorem_name>.md`
54	   - In your `task_results/<file>.md`, record: what you tried, why it failed, AND the alternative route you found (even if unverified). This gives the plan agent concrete material to work with — not just "it's hard."
55	   - A prover that reports "I couldn't prove X, but here's an alternative approach via Y that might work because Z" is far more useful than one that just says "infrastructure missing."
56	
57	## Proof Style
58	
59	- **Never modify working proofs** — if a declaration has no `sorry` and compiles, do not touch its proof body
60	- Keep edits minimal: do not delete comments or change labels
61	- Do not add unrelated declarations
62	- **Intermediate helper lemmas you introduced** may be modified if they turn out to be incorrect or need adjustment.
63	- Add concise, informative comments above helper lemmas to make later reuse easy
64	
65	## Interpreting Mathlib Tags in PROGRESS.md
66	
67	The Plan Agent will tag suggested Mathlib lemmas to indicate its confidence. Adjust your effort accordingly:
68	- [verified] — The Plan Agent confirmed this exists. 
69	- [expected] — The Plan Agent is guessing this name based on conventions. Do not assume it exists; do a quick `lean_local_search` and pivot immediately if it doesn't.
70	- [gap] — The Plan Agent verified this does NOT exist in Mathlib. Do not waste time searching for it; formalize a workaround or helper lemma.
71	
72	## LSP MCP Tools: Invocation Rules
73	
74	The `archon-lean-lsp` server exposes Lean LSP operations as **MCP tool calls**, not shell commands. Their full names start with `mcp__archon-lean-lsp__` (e.g. `mcp__archon-lean-lsp__lean_goal`, `mcp__archon-lean-lsp__lean_diagnostic_messages`, `mcp__archon-lean-lsp__lean_local_search`). The lean4 skill reference uses the short names (`lean_goal`, `lean_local_search`, …) for readability — those are the **same tools**, never standalone shell binaries.
75	
76	- **Always invoke them through your tool-call interface**, the same way you call `Read` or `Grep`.
77	- **Never** call `Bash` with `lean_goal …` / `lean_diagnostic_messages …` — there is no such shell command and it will fail with `command not found`.
78	- As your **first LSP action**, call `mcp__archon-lean-lsp__lean_diagnostic_messages` on your assigned file. If it returns `success: false` the server is cold; retry once or run `lake build` once via Bash, then retry.
79	
80	## Search Protocol
81	
82	Follow the search tool priority and query guidance in the lean4 skill reference (`references/lean-lsp-tools-api.md`). Key points:
83	
84	1. `lean_local_search` first
85	2. `lean_leansearch` for semantic search — **describe the mathematical content**, not just the name
86	3. `lean_loogle` for simple type patterns only
87	4. Never use local file search (find, grep) to locate Mathlib theorems
88	
89	## Missing Lemmas & Impossibility
90	
91	Follow the lean4 skill reference (`references/sorry-filling.md`) for:
92	- **When Mathlib lacks a theorem**: bypass or implement yourself. Web Search for published papers. Never leave a `sorry` just because Mathlib doesn't have it.
93	- **Distinguish impossibility from difficulty**: technical difficulty → keep trying. Mathematical impossibility → immediately backtrack and document why.
94	
95	## Tooling Traps & Verification
96	
97	- Trust `goals_after`, not just empty diagnostics: When using `lean_multi_attempt`, do not assume a snippet worked just because `diagnostics: []` is empty. Line-scoping quirks mean errors sometimes attach to the enclosing by block. You MUST check that goals_after is empty (or has advanced) to confirm success.
98	- Beware `lean_run_code` with imports: Standalone `lean_run_code` snippets can sometimes silently swallow elaboration errors if they contain import statements. Rely on the actual file's `lean_diagnostic_messages` for authoritative verification.
99	
100	## Logging
101	
102	Write your results to `task_results/<your_file>.md`. Use the file name from your assigned `.lean` file (e.g., if you own `Algebra/WLocal.lean`, write to `task_results/Algebra_WLocal.lean.md`).
103	
104	**Format:**
105	
106	```markdown
107	# Algebra/WLocal.lean
108	
109	## wLocal_iff (line 45)
110	### Attempt 1
111	- **Approach:** Direct case split on maximal ideals
112	- **Result:** FAILED — needed IsLocalRing instance not available
113	- **Dead end:** Do not try direct case split without IsLocalRing
114	
115	### Attempt 2
116	- **Approach:** Use Stacks 0A31, characterize via bijection on spectra
117	- **Result:** RESOLVED
118	- **Key insight:** Mathlib's PrimeSpectrum.comap_injective bridges the gap
119	
120	## helper_bijective (line 78)
121	### Attempt 1
122	- **Approach:** Split conjunction into injectivity and surjectivity. Prove injectivity.
123	- **Result:** PARTIAL (Branch Closed) — Injectivity closed. Sorry count unchanged because surjectivity remains.
124	- **Next step:** Try PrimeSpectrum.range_comap_of_surjective for the right branch.
125	- **Relevant lemmas found:** PrimeSpectrum.comap_surjective
126	```
127	
128	**Rules:**
129	1. One section per theorem/lemma in your file
130	2. Each attempt records: approach, result (RESOLVED / FAILED / IN PROGRESS), dead-end warnings or next steps
131	3. Log negative search results (e.g., "Searched 'projective module infinite rank' — nothing in Mathlib")
132	4. The plan agent collects these files and merges them into `task_pending.md` / `task_done.md`
133	
134	**Read-only references:** Read `task_pending.md` for prior context on your file. Read `task_done.md` if the current problem resembles a completed one. Do not write to either.
135	
136	## Summary Pipeline
137	
138	1. Read `task_pending.md` and `task_done.md` for context from prior sessions
139	2. Read the informal proof / blueprint to understand the proof strategy and lemma decomposition
140	3. Introduce helper lemmas (matching the blueprint's structure) in the `.lean` file
141	4. Replace `sorry` placeholders with complete proofs, ensuring the file compiles without errors
142	5. Do not modify protected declarations listed in `archon-protected.yaml`.
143	6. Use Mathlib theorems when possible. Use Web Search when Mathlib lacks referenced results
144	7. Rely on Lean LSP for diagnostics; use `lake env lean <file>` sparingly for final checks
145	8. Log all explorations in `task_results/<your_file>.md`
146	
147	## End-of-session handoff
148	
149	Before you stop (or when you are done with this round of work):
150	
151	1. Write to `task_results/<your_file>.md` with:
152	   - Current result (IN PROGRESS / FAILED) and what you tried
153	   - Any Mathlib lemmas you discovered that are relevant
154	   - Concrete next step for the next session
155	   - Dead-end warnings for approaches that won't work
156	2. Save all file changes (ensure compilation passes, using scoped `sorry` if needed)
157

2

6	lane; close Steps 1c–4 of `h_loc_exact` at L1802 of `BasicOpenCech.lean`,

16	
17	## Stages
18	- [x] init
19	- [x] autoformalize
20	- [ ] prover
21	- [ ] polish
22	
23	## Big-picture context
24	
25	This project formalizes the Jacobian of a smooth proper geometrically irreducible
26	curve over a field, following Christian Merten's challenge (`references/challenge.lean`).
27	The nine declarations in `archon-protected.yaml` are the deliverables.
28

142	#### Step 3 — `IsLocalizedModule.pi` on the finite product
143	
144	Apply [verified] `IsLocalizedModule.pi`
145	(`Mathlib.RingTheory.TensorProduct.IsBaseChangePi`). Signature:
146	```
147	instance pi {ι : Type*} [Finite ι] {M M' : ι → Type*}
148	    [∀ i, AddCommMonoid (M i)] [∀ i, AddCommMonoid (M' i)]
149	    [∀ i, Module R (M i)] [∀ i, Module R (M' i)]
150	    (f : ∀ i, M i →ₗ[R] M' i) [∀ i, IsLocalizedModule S (f i)] :
151	    IsLocalizedModule S (LinearMap.pi fun i ↦ f i ∘ₗ LinearMap.proj i)
152	```
153	
154	`Fin (n + 1) → ↑s₀` is `Finite` via [expected] `Fintype.toFinite`;
155	the per-coord `IsLocalizedModule (powers f.1)` from Step 2 packages
156	into the product instance for the LinearMap.pi of restrictions.

158	#### Step 4 — `IsLocalizedModule.iso` + `Function.Exact.iff_of_ladder_linearEquiv`
159	
160	[verified] `IsLocalizedModule.iso (powers f.1) (LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap _ scK₀.X_i)`
161	gives `LocalizedModule (powers f.1) scK₀.X_i ≃ₗ[R] M_i'` where `M_i'` is
162	the slice-cover degree-i term modulo the `ModuleCat.piIsoPi` (already
163	in scope as `e_i` at L1615–L1617) repackaging. Compose with `e_i.symm`
164	to get an R-linear equiv `LocalizedModule (powers f.1) scK₀.X_i ≃ₗ[R]
165	slice_cover.X_i`.
166	
167	Apply [verified] `Function.Exact.iff_of_ladder_linearEquiv`
168	(`Mathlib.Algebra.Exact`):
169	```
170	theorem Function.Exact.iff_of_ladder_linearEquiv
171	    {f₁₂ : M →ₗ[R] N} {f₂₃ : N →ₗ[R] P} {g₁₂ : M' →ₗ[R] N'} {g₂₃ : N' →ₗ[R] P'}
172	    (e_M : M ≃ₗ[R] M') (e_N : N ≃ₗ[R] N') (e_P : P ≃ₗ[R] P')
173	    (h_left : e_N ∘ₗ f₁₂ = g₁₂ ∘ₗ e_M) (h_right : e_P ∘ₗ f₂₃ = g₂₃ ∘ₗ e_N) :
174	    Function.Exact f₁₂ f₂₃ ↔ Function.Exact g₁₂ g₂₃
175	```
176	
177	The two commutation squares (`h_left`, `h_right`) come from uniqueness
178	of `IsLocalizedModule.map` via [verified] `IsLocalizedModule.linearMap_ext`
179	(both sides agree on the image of `LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap`, which
180	generates by the universal property).
181	
182	The RHS `Function.Exact g₁₂ g₂₃` is the slice-cover degree-n exactness,
183	which is `h_a₀_fun f` already in scope around L1565+.

37	/-- Iter-054: basic-open cover from a spanning subset of `Γ(C.left, U)`.
38	
39	Given a subset `s ⊆ Γ(C.left, U)`, this constructs the family of basic opens
40	`s → Opens` mapping each `f ∈ s` to `C.left.basicOpen f`. When `s` spans the
41	unit ideal of `Γ(C.left, U)` and `U` is affine, this family covers `U`
42	(see `basicOpenCover_supr_of_span_eq_top`); each member is itself an affine
43	open (see `basicOpenCover_isAffineOpen`).
44	
45	Iter-054 is a thin Mathlib-API wrapper (parameterised by the project's
46	`C : Over (Spec (.of k))` convention) so iter-055+ instantiation work for
47	`HasAffineCechAcyclicCover (toModuleKSheaf C)` can call the constructor +
48	supremum + affine-membership directly.
49	
50	Mirrors the iter-047 foundational parameterised Čech infrastructure pattern:
51	package thin Mathlib wrappers as named project-local declarations so the
52	substantive multi-iteration work downstream focuses purely on content
53	(Koszul acyclicity + Čech-vs-derived comparison) rather than re-deriving
54	infrastructure at every call site. -/
55	noncomputable def basicOpenCover
56	    {k : Type u} [Field k] {C : Over (Spec (.of k))}
57	    {U : TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat}
58	    (s : Set Γ(C.left, U)) :
59	    s → TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat :=
60	  fun f => C.left.basicOpen (f : Γ(C.left, U))
61	
62	/-- Iter-054: supremum equality for the basic-open cover from generators
63	spanning the unit ideal of an affine open.
64	
65	Given `(hU : IsAffineOpen U)` and `(hs : Ideal.span s = ⊤)`, the basic-open
66	cover `basicOpenCover s` covers `U` exactly: `⨆ i, basicOpenCover s i = U`.
67	This is a thin wrapper around Mathlib's `IsAffineOpen.iSup_basicOpen_eq_self_iff`
68	(`Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/AffineScheme.lean` L898).
69	
70	Used by iter-055+ when constructing the `HasAffineCechAcyclicCover (toModuleKSheaf C)`
71	instance: the existential's third conjunct `(⨆ i, 𝒰 i = U)` is exactly what
72	this theorem produces. -/
73	theorem basicOpenCover_supr_of_span_eq_top
74	    {k : Type u} [Field k] {C : Over (Spec (.of k))}
75	    {U : TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat} (hU : IsAffineOpen U)
76	    (s : Set Γ(C.left, U)) (hs : Ideal.span s = ⊤) :
77	    ⨆ i, basicOpenCover (C := C) (U := U) s i = U := by
78	  unfold basicOpenCover
79	  exact hU.iSup_basicOpen_eq_self_iff.mpr hs
80	
81	/-- Iter-054: each member of the basic-open cover of an affine open is itself
82	affine.
83	
84	Given `(hU : IsAffineOpen U)` and `(i : s)`, conclude `IsAffineOpen (basicOpenCover s i)`.
85	This is a thin wrapper around Mathlib's `IsAffineOpen.basicOpen`
86	(`Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/AffineScheme.lean` L589).
87	
88	Used by iter-055+ when proving the Koszul acyclicity of the basic-open Čech
89	complex: each finite intersection of basic opens is itself basic (hence affine),
90	which is the inductive step for the Koszul-style alternating-sum argument. -/
91	theorem basicOpenCover_isAffineOpen
92	    {k : Type u} [Field k] {C : Over (Spec (.of k))}
93	    {U : TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat} (hU : IsAffineOpen U)
94	    (s : Set Γ(C.left, U)) (i : s) :
95	    IsAffineOpen (basicOpenCover (C := C) (U := U) s i) := by
96	  unfold basicOpenCover
97	  exact hU.basicOpen i.1
98	
99	/-- Iter-055: producer for `HasAffineCechAcyclicCover` from existence of basic-open
100	Čech-acyclic cover.
101	
102	Given that for every affine open `U` of `C.left` there exists a spanning subset
103	`s ⊆ Γ(C.left, U)` with `Ideal.span s = ⊤` such that the basic-open cover
104	`basicOpenCover s : s → Opens` carries both `IsCechAcyclicCover F (basicOpenCover s)`
105	and `HasCechToHModuleIso F (basicOpenCover s)`, conclude `HasAffineCechAcyclicCover F`.
106	
107	Iter-055 specialises iter-053's existence-bundled cover form to the basic-open cover
108	shape constructed iter-054. Strategic value: factors out the existence-bundling step
109	from iter-056+ substantive work, so the iter-056+ Koszul acyclicity branch and the
110	iter-057+ Čech-vs-derived comparison branch can each prove specifically the basic-open
111	form (which the universal Koszul + comparison arguments naturally produce), and the
112	iter-055 producer then bundles their results into iter-053's carrier shape automatically.
113	
114	Body: destructures the per-affine existence to obtain `(s, hs, hacy, hcomp)`, then
115	bundles them into iter-053's existential cover via iter-054's
116	`basicOpenCover_supr_of_span_eq_top` to produce the supremum hypothesis. -/
117	theorem hasAffineCechAcyclicCover_of_basicOpen
118	    {k : Type u} [Field k] {C : Over (Spec (.of k))}
119	    {F : Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.{u} k)}
120	    [HasExt.{u} (Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.{u} k))]

1409	      (basicOpenCover (C := C) (U := U) (↑s₀ : Set Γ(C.left, U)))

1413	    -- ⨆ basicOpenCover ↑s₀`).  The transport is stated as a `have` below;
1414	    -- closing it requires either a direct homological-algebra argument

1419	      Function.Exact

1431	      -- property of the categorical product, this gives a projection map
1432	      -- `π_i : K.X i → K₀.X i` in `ModuleCat k` (project onto the smaller index
1433	      -- set).  These assemble into a cochain-complex map `π : K → K₀` because
1434	      -- the Čech differential is natural in the index set.
1435	      --
1436	      -- **Iter-065 concrete construction** of `π`.  We assemble it through the
1437	      -- Mathlib API:

1439	      --      `g_FC : ⟨↑s₀, basicOpenCover ↑s₀⟩ ⟶ ⟨s, basicOpenCover s⟩`

1462	              (basicOpenCover (C := C) (U := U)

1464	          (Limits.FormalCoproduct.mk s

1519	          funext i_1
1520	          erw [Limits.Pi.map_id, CategoryTheory.op_id, CategoryTheory.Functor.map_id,
1521	            CategoryTheory.Category.comp_id]
1522	      -- Step 3: the kernel of `π` at degree `i` is the product of the factors
1523	      -- indexed by `x : Fin (i+1) → s` that are *not* in the image of
1524	      -- `Fin (i+1) → ↑s₀`.  The kernel complex is acyclic in positive degrees
1525	      -- because it is a Čech complex of a cover that is a refinement of a cover
1526	      -- with a terminal object (the extra-degeneracy argument applies to the
1527	      -- kernel once `s` is reduced modulo `↑s₀`).  Alternatively, one can use
1528	      -- the sheaf-theoretic fact that Čech cohomology depends only on the
1529	      -- generated sieve, and both `s` and `s₀` generate the same sieve `U`.
1530	      --
1531	      -- Step 4: from the short exact sequence of complexes
1532	      -- `0 → ker(π) → K → K₀ → 0` and exactness of `K₀` at degree `n`, the
1533	      -- long exact sequence in cohomology gives exactness of `K` at degree `n`.
1534	      -- A direct diagram-chase argument (or `HomologicalComplex.homologyMap`)
1535	      -- closes the goal.
1536	      sorry
1537	    -- ===========================================================================
1538	    -- Iter-064: component (ii) scaffolding — `exact_of_isLocalized_span` on `s₀`.
1539	    -- ===========================================================================

Updated task #1 status

200	cover members `basicOpenCover s i` for `i ∈ t` equals the basic open of the
201	finite product `∏ i ∈ t, i.1` in `Γ(C.left, U)`. Generalises iter-056's binary
202	`basicOpenCover_inter_eq_basicOpen_mul` to arbitrary non-empty Finset indexing.
203	
204	Body via `Finset.cons_induction`: the empty case is impossible by `t.Nonempty`
205	(closed via `Finset.not_nonempty_empty`); the cons case splits on whether the
206	residual `t` is empty or non-empty (via `Finset.eq_empty_or_nonempty`). In the
207	empty residual case, `simp [basicOpenCover]` closes via single-element reduction
208	of `Finset.inf'` and `Finset.prod`. In the non-empty residual case, decompose
209	via `Finset.inf'_cons hne` to expose `basicOpenCover s a ⊓ rest`, decompose
210	`Finset.prod_cons` to expose `a.1 * (∏ i ∈ t, i.1)`, apply the IH to identify
211	`rest = C.left.basicOpen (∏ i ∈ t, i.1)`, then `change ... ⊓ _ = _` followed by
212	`rw [← Scheme.basicOpen_mul]` consolidates the product form.
213	
214	This is the foundational identification for iter-058+ Čech complex evaluation
215	at arbitrary degree `n`: the Čech cochain at degree `n` indexes `x : Fin (n+1) → s`
216	and assigns the sheaf evaluated at `⨅ i : Fin (n+1), basicOpenCover s (x i)`.
217	Via this lemma (instantiated at `t := (Finset.univ : Finset (Fin (n+1))).image x`,
218	or more directly via `Finset.univ.inf' Finset.univ_nonempty (basicOpenCover s ∘ x)`),
219	that intersection identifies with `C.left.basicOpen (∏ i : Fin (n+1), (x i).1)`,
220	which by `IsAffineOpen.isLocalization_of_eq_basicOpen` (Mathlib) is the
221	localization `Localization.Away (∏ i, (x i).1)` of `Γ(C.left, U)`. -/
222	theorem basicOpenCover_finset_inf'_eq_basicOpen_prod
223	    {k : Type u} [Field k] {C : Over (Spec (.of k))}
224	    {U : TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat}
225	    (s : Set Γ(C.left, U)) (t : Finset s) (h : t.Nonempty) :
226	    t.inf' h (basicOpenCover (C := C) (U := U) s) =
227	      C.left.basicOpen (∏ i ∈ t, (i.1 : Γ(C.left, U))) := by
228	  classical
229	  induction t using Finset.cons_induction with
230	  | empty => exact absurd h Finset.not_nonempty_empty
231	  | cons a t ha ih =>
232	    rcases t.eq_empty_or_nonempty with rfl | hne
233	    · simp [basicOpenCover]
234	    · rw [Finset.inf'_cons hne, Finset.prod_cons, ih hne]
235	      change C.left.basicOpen (a.1 : Γ(C.left, U)) ⊓ _ = _
236	      rw [← Scheme.basicOpen_mul]
237	
238	/-- Iter-057: n-ary basic-open intersection on an affine open is itself affine.
239	
240	Given `(hU : IsAffineOpen U)`, `(s : Set Γ(C.left, U))`, `(t : Finset s)`, and
241	`(h : t.Nonempty)`, the n-ary intersection `t.inf' h (basicOpenCover s)` is
242	itself affine. Term-mode body chains
243	`basicOpenCover_finset_inf'_eq_basicOpen_prod` (rewriting the n-ary intersection
244	as a basic open of the finite product) with Mathlib's `IsAffineOpen.basicOpen`
245	(membership preservation under basic-open formation).
246	
247	Generalises iter-056's `basicOpenCover_inter_isAffineOpen` (pairwise affine
248	membership) to arbitrary non-empty Finset indexing. Used by iter-058+ Čech
249	evaluation: each finite intersection of basic opens of an affine is itself
250	affine, which is the inductive step for the Koszul alternating-sum argument
251	(Stacks 03F7 / Hartshorne III.4.5 / Eisenbud Commutative Algebra §17 Koszul
252	resolution).
253	
254	**Body uses `▸` (Eq.mpr), NOT `rw`**: `rw` on the equation produced by Theorem
255	(1) fails because of motive-occurrence issues with the implicit `Subtype.val`
256	coercions on elements of `Finset s`; term-mode `▸` rewrite resolves these
257	directly without invoking the rewriter's pattern-matching. Plan-agent
258	`lean_run_code` probe (this pass) confirmed the term-mode form compiles
259	kernel-only while the tactic-mode `rw` form fails with "Did not find an
260	occurrence of the pattern" error. -/
261	theorem basicOpenCover_finset_inf'_isAffineOpen
262	    {k : Type u} [Field k] {C : Over (Spec (.of k))}
263	    {U : TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat} (hU : IsAffineOpen U)
264	    (s : Set Γ(C.left, U)) (t : Finset s) (h : t.Nonempty) :
265	    IsAffineOpen (t.inf' h (basicOpenCover (C := C) (U := U) s)) :=
266	  basicOpenCover_finset_inf'_eq_basicOpen_prod s t h ▸ hU.basicOpen _
267	
268	/-- Iter-058: n-ary basic-open intersection is contained in `U`.
269	
270	Given a non-empty `Finset t : Finset s`, the infimum (over `t`) of the basic-open
271	cover members `basicOpenCover s i` for `i ∈ t` is contained in `U`. Term-mode
272	body chains iter-057's `basicOpenCover_finset_inf'_eq_basicOpen_prod` (rewriting
273	the n-ary intersection as a single basic open of the finite product) with
274	Mathlib's `Scheme.basicOpen_le` (`X.basicOpen f ≤ U` for any `f ∈ Γ(X, U)`)
275	via Lean's `▸` (Eq.mpr) rewrite.
276	
277	**No `IsAffineOpen` hypothesis required**: `basicOpen_le` holds for any open `U`
278	(the basic open of `f ∈ Γ(X, U)` is always a sub-open of `U`); compare iter-057's
279	`basicOpenCover_finset_inf'_isAffineOpen` which does need `(hU : IsAffineOpen U)`.
280	
281	This is the inclusion morphism iter-059+ Čech-evaluation arguments need when
282	applying Mathlib's `IsAffineOpen.isLocalization_of_eq_basicOpen`:
283	```
284	(hU : IsAffineOpen U) (f : Γ(X, U)) {V : X.Opens} (i : V ⟶ U) :
285	  V = X.basicOpen f → IsLocalization.Away f Γ(X, V)
286	```
287	The morphism `i : V ⟶ U` argument (which is `homOfLE` of `V ≤ U`) is needed to
288	install the `Algebra Γ(X, U) Γ(X, V)` instance for the conclusion type, since
289	Mathlib's `algebra_section_section_basicOpen` instance only fires for the
290	syntactic form `Γ(X, X.basicOpen f)`, not for `V = t.inf' h (basicOpenCover s)`.
291	Iter-058's inclusion lemma supplies this `V ≤ U` proof so iter-059+ can call
292	`hU.isLocalization_of_eq_basicOpen f (homOfLE (basicOpenCover_finset_inf'_le ...))
293	(basicOpenCover_finset_inf'_eq_basicOpen_prod ...)` to land
294	`IsLocalization.Away (∏ i ∈ t, i.1) Γ(C.left, t.inf' h (basicOpenCover s))`
295	directly. -/
296	theorem basicOpenCover_finset_inf'_le
297	    {k : Type u} [Field k] {C : Over (Spec (.of k))}
298	    {U : TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat}
299	    (s : Set Γ(C.left, U)) (t : Finset s) (h : t.Nonempty) :
300	    t.inf' h (basicOpenCover (C := C) (U := U) s) ≤ U :=
301	  basicOpenCover_finset_inf'_eq_basicOpen_prod s t h ▸
302	    C.left.basicOpen_le _
303	
304	/-- Iter-059: n-ary basic-open intersection is a localization.
305	
306	Given an affine open `U`, a subset `s ⊆ Γ(C.left, U)`, and a non-empty `Finset t : Finset s`,
307	the structure sheaf evaluated on the n-ary intersection of basic-open cover members
308	`t.inf' h (basicOpenCover s)` is identified — as a localization away from the product
309	`∏ i ∈ t, i.1` of generators — with `Γ(C.left, U)`. The algebra structure on the conclusion
310	type is given explicitly by `(C.left.presheaf.map (homOfLE _).op).hom.toAlgebra`, threaded
311	through the conclusion `@IsLocalization.Away _ _ f Γ(X, V) _ algebra` form (matching
312	Mathlib's `IsAffineOpen.isLocalization_of_eq_basicOpen` signature exactly).
313	
314	**Explicit `@`-algebra binding is essential.** Mathlib's default
315	`algebra_section_section_basicOpen` instance (`Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/Scheme.lean` L724)
316	fires only for the syntactic form `Γ(X, X.basicOpen f)`, *not* for arbitrary `V`
317	definitionally equal to `X.basicOpen f`. The iter-058 plan-agent probe-confirmed that
318	stating this theorem with *inferred* algebra fails with
319	`failed to synthesize instance of type class Algebra Γ(C.left, U) Γ(C.left, t.inf' h …)`.
320	Threading the algebra structure as part of the conclusion type — matching Mathlib's
321	signature exactly — sidesteps the synthesis attempt.
322	
323	This is the **fourth and final** thin foundational scaffolding helper on the affine
324	basic-open identification side. The iter-060+ basic-open Koszul acyclicity argument
325	(per the iter-059 analogy report at `analogies/cech-koszul-precedent.md`, Branch A
326	substep 2) chains iter-057's equality + iter-058's inclusion + iter-059's localization
327	+ `IsLocalizedModule.map_exact` (`Mathlib/Algebra/Module/LocalizedModule/Exact.lean`)
328	to identify each Čech-cochain factor `F(⋂_k 𝒰(x k))` with the cover-of-`D(f)` Čech complex
329	on localised modules. -/
330	theorem basicOpenCover_finset_inf'_isLocalization
331	    {k : Type u} [Field k] {C : Over (Spec (.of k))}
332	    {U : TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat} (hU : IsAffineOpen U)
333	    (s : Set Γ(C.left, U)) (t : Finset s) (h : t.Nonempty) :
334	    @IsLocalization.Away _ _ (∏ i ∈ t, (i.1 : Γ(C.left, U)))
335	      Γ(C.left, t.inf' h (basicOpenCover (C := C) (U := U) s)) _
336	      ((C.left.presheaf.map (homOfLE
337	        (basicOpenCover_finset_inf'_le (C := C) (U := U) s t h)).op).hom.toAlgebra) :=
338	  hU.isLocalization_of_eq_basicOpen _
339	    (homOfLE (basicOpenCover_finset_inf'_le (C := C) (U := U) s t h))
340	    (basicOpenCover_finset_inf'_eq_basicOpen_prod (C := C) (U := U) s t h)
341	
342	/-- Iter-066 helper: in `ModuleCat k`, a product projection induced by an
343	injection of index sets is a split epi.
344	
345	Given an injection `f : β → α` and a family `M : α → ModuleCat k`, the natural
346	projection `∏ᶜ_a M a ⟶ ∏ᶜ_b M (f b)` defined as `Pi.lift (fun b => Pi.π M (f b))`
347	(which sends a tuple indexed by `α` to its `image f` components, reindexed by `β`)
348	admits a section in `ModuleCat k`. The section is the "extend by zero" map:
349	given a tuple indexed by `β`, fill in the components in `image f` (using the
350	unique preimage given by injectivity of `f`) and set the remaining components
351	to `0`.
352	
353	This is the categorical fact underlying `h_π_split` in
354	`basicOpenCover_isCechAcyclicCover_toModuleKSheaf`: the cochain-complex map
355	`π : K ⟶ K₀` is at each degree of this form (the `↑s₀ ↪ s` inclusion induces a
356	postcomposition map `(Fin (i+1) → ↑s₀) → (Fin (i+1) → s)` which is injective by
357	injectivity of the inclusion, hence the induced product projection on cochain
358	factors is split).
359	
360	The helper is stated for a generic injection so it can be reused for both the
361	`s` substep (`h_π_split` proper) and the slice-cover variants. -/
362	noncomputable def splitEpi_pi_lift_of_injective {k : Type u} [Field k]
363	    {α β : Type u} (M : α → ModuleCat.{u} k)
364	    (f : β → α) (hf : Function.Injective f) :
365	    SplitEpi (Pi.lift (fun b : β => Pi.π M (f b))) where
366	  section_ := by
367	    classical
368	    refine Pi.lift (fun a => ?_)
369	    by_cases h : ∃ b, f b = a
370	    · exact h.choose_spec ▸ Pi.π (fun b => M (f b)) h.choose
371	    · exact 0
372	  id := by
373	    classical
374	    apply Pi.hom_ext
375	    intro b
376	    rw [Category.assoc, Pi.lift_π, Category.id_comp, Pi.lift_π]
377	    have h : ∃ b', f b' = f b := ⟨b, rfl⟩
378	    rw [dif_pos h]
379	    -- The transport `h.choose_spec ▸ Pi.π _ h.choose = Pi.π _ b`.
380	    -- By injectivity of `f`, `h.choose = b`, so the transport collapses.
381	    -- We use the helper lemma below.
382	    have key : ∀ {b b' : β} (heq : f b' = f b),
383	        heq ▸ (Pi.π (fun b'' => M (f b'')) b' :
384	            (∏ᶜ fun b'' => M (f b'')) ⟶ M (f b')) =
385	          (Pi.π (fun b'' => M (f b'')) b :
386	            (∏ᶜ fun b'' => M (f b'')) ⟶ M (f b)) := by
387	      intro b b' heq
388	      have hbb : b' = b := hf heq
389	      subst hbb
390	      rfl
391	    exact key h.choose_spec
392	
393	/-- Iter-060 helper: bridge from `ExactAt n` on the Čech cochain complex to
394	`Subsingleton (cechCohomology n)`.
395	
396	The chain is two steps: `HomologicalComplex.ExactAt.isZero_homology` packages an
397	`ExactAt n` hypothesis into `IsZero ((cechCochain ...).homology n)`, and
398	`ModuleCat.subsingleton_of_isZero` then upgrades `IsZero` on a `ModuleCat`
399	object to `Subsingleton` on its underlying carrier type.
400	
401	This is the structural skeleton common to *every* `IsCechAcyclicCover` instance
402	proof: the actual mathematical content lives in establishing
403	`(cechCochain C F 𝒰).ExactAt n` for each `n > 0`. -/
404	theorem cechCohomology_subsingleton_of_cechCochain_exactAt
405	    {k : Type u} [Field k] {C : Over (Spec (.of k))}
406	    {F : Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.{u} k)}
407	    {ι : Type u} {𝒰 : ι → TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat}
408	    {n : ℕ} (h : (cechCochain C F 𝒰).ExactAt n) :
409	    Subsingleton (cechCohomology C F 𝒰 n) :=
410	  ModuleCat.subsingleton_of_isZero h.isZero_homology
411	
412	/-- Iter-087 (refactor): per-summand R-linear restriction-collapse. For nested
413	opens `V ≤ W ≤ U` in a scheme `C.left`, the chain of presheaf restrictions
414	`Γ(U) → Γ(W) → Γ(V)` collapses to the direct restriction `Γ(U) → Γ(V)`.
415	
416	Used in the proof of R-linearity of the Čech differential
417	(`cechCofaceMap_pi_smul`): each per-summand restriction chain
418	`R = Γ(C.left, U) → Γ(C.left, V_{j∘δ_i}) → Γ(C.left, V_j)` collapses to the
419	direct map `R → Γ(C.left, V_j)`, matching the per-i R-action induced by the
420	restriction algebra structure on the cochain factor.

422	Extracted iter-087 from the inline `have R_restrict_R_linear` (iter-086) inside
423	`basicOpenCover_isCechAcyclicCover_toModuleKSheaf`. The body is the
424	2-tactic proof verified by the iter-086 prover. -/
425	lemma presheafMap_restrict_collapse
426	    {k : Type u} [Field k] {C : Over (Spec (.of k))}
427	    {V W U : TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat}
428	    (h_VW : V ≤ W) (h_VU : V ≤ U) (h_WU : W ≤ U)
429	    (r : C.left.presheaf.obj (Opposite.op U)) :
430	    (C.left.presheaf.map (homOfLE h_VW).op).hom
431	        ((C.left.presheaf.map (homOfLE h_WU).op).hom r) =
432	      (C.left.presheaf.map (homOfLE h_VU).op).hom r := by
433	  rw [← ConcreteCategory.comp_apply, ← C.left.presheaf.map_comp]
434	  congr 1
435	
436	/-- Iter-102 refactor (cechcoface-named-family): extracted top-level family for the
437	sign-free Čech coface morphism components. The body is the `Pi.lift fun i_1 ↦ ...`
438	expression that appears (anonymously) in the post-iter-099 expanded
439	Čech differential inside `cechCofaceMap_pi_smul`.

442	an anonymous closure, which unblocks the iter-099 `_sum_comp` /
443	`alternating_zsmul_pi_smul_aux_sum_comp` call-site application:
444	- Miller unification on the G slot becomes trivial (G is given explicitly).
445	- The per-summand R-linearity hypothesis is a separate top-level theorem
446	  (`cechCofaceMap_summand_family_R_linear` below) that proves R-linearity
447	  of the named family at the binder level (HOU-free).

449	The codomain index `Fin ((ComplexShape.up ℕ).prev n + 2) → ↑s₀` keeps the
450	definition independent of `hn : 0 < n` — it is the natural domain of
451	`(SimplexCategory.δ i).toOrderHom` for `i : Fin ((ComplexShape.up ℕ).prev n + 2)`.
452	The call site bridges to `Fin (n + 1) → ↑s₀` via the existing `eqToHom`
453	slot fed into `E` of `alternating_zsmul_pi_smul_aux_sum_comp`. -/
454	noncomputable def cechCofaceMap_summand_family
455	    {k : Type u} [Field k] {C : Over (Spec (.of k))}
456	    {U : TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat}
457	    (s₀ : Finset Γ(C.left, U)) (n : ℕ)
458	    (i : Fin ((ComplexShape.up ℕ).prev n + 2)) :
459	    (∏ᶜ fun j : Fin ((ComplexShape.up ℕ).prev n + 1) → ↑s₀ =>
460	        ModuleCat.of k

462	            (Opposite.op (∏ᶜ fun a => basicOpenCover (C := C) (U := U) ↑s₀ (j a))))) ⟶
463	    (∏ᶜ fun j : Fin ((ComplexShape.up ℕ).prev n + 2) → ↑s₀ =>

466	            (Opposite.op (∏ᶜ fun a => basicOpenCover (C := C) (U := U) ↑s₀ (j a))))) :=
467	  Pi.lift fun i_1 : Fin ((ComplexShape.up ℕ).prev n + 2) → ↑s₀ =>
468	    Pi.π (fun j : Fin ((ComplexShape.up ℕ).prev n + 1) → ↑s₀ =>
469	            ModuleCat.of k

1129	/-- Iter-060 (Phase A step 6 / Path 2 — **substantive Čech acyclicity attempt**):
1130	the basic-open cover of an affine open `U` by a spanning subset `s ⊆ Γ(C.left, U)`
1131	gives a Čech-acyclic cover for the `ModuleCat k`-valued structure sheaf
1132	`toModuleKSheaf C`. This is the Stacks 01ED Čech-acyclicity theorem for affine
1133	schemes in our setting.
1134	
1135	**Mathematical content (Stacks 01ED route).** For each positive degree `n`,
1136	exactness of the Čech cochain at degree `n` is a *local* property on the
1137	spanning set `s`. Localising the Čech complex at any `f ∈ s` exhibits the
1138	slice over `D(f)` as a Čech complex of an affine cover whose terminal vertex
1139	`D(f) → D(f)` supplies an *extra degeneracy* (Mathlib's
1140	`FormalCoproduct.extraDegeneracyCech`). By
1141	`SimplicialObject.Augmented.ExtraDegeneracy.homotopyEquiv` the augmented
1142	chain complex of the slice is homotopy equivalent to the constant complex on
1143	the augmentation, hence exact in positive degrees. Re-globalising via
1144	`exact_of_isLocalized_span` (Mathlib `RingTheory/LocalProperties/Exactness.lean`
1145	L173) yields exactness of the *unlocalised* Čech complex at degree `n`.
1146	
1147	The structural skeleton is:
1148	* reduce `Subsingleton (cechCohomology n)` to `ExactAt (cechCochain) n` via
1149	  `cechCohomology_subsingleton_of_cechCochain_exactAt`;
1150	* the substantive `ExactAt n` is decomposed into three substeps tracked by
1151	  separate `sorry`s, each annotated with the missing computational step.
1152	
1153	**Transient sorries (iter-060)** — explicitly endorsed by the iteration
1154	directive; each sorry names a substep. The substeps are:
1155	  (a) **local exactness at `D(f)`** (extra-degeneracy invocation): given the
1156	      cover-of-`D(f)` slice, build the augmented Čech simplicial object,
1157	      apply `FormalCoproduct.extraDegeneracyCech` to obtain an
1158	      `ExtraDegeneracy`, then transport via
1159	      `ExtraDegeneracy.homotopyEquiv` and op-passage `CochainComplex.opEquivalence`
1160	      to get cochain-level exactness in positive degrees;
1161	  (b) **localised-Čech identification**: the localisation at `f` of the
1162	      degree-`n` Čech cochain is the degree-`n` cochain of the cover-of-`D(f)`
1163	      Čech complex, via iter-057+ identifications chained with
1164	      `IsLocalizedModule.map_exact`;
1165	  (c) **local-to-global** via `exact_of_isLocalized_span`: the assembled
1166	      family of per-`f` local exactnesses lifts to global exactness.
1167	
1168	The decomposition follows §"Branch A — Čech acyclicity" of
1169	`analogies/cech-koszul-precedent.md`. -/

{"symbol":"omega","info":"The `omega` tactic, for resolving integer and natural linear arithmetic problems.

It is not yet a full decision procedure (no "dark" or "grey" shadows),
but should be effective on many problems.

We handle hypotheses of the form `x = y`, `x < y`, `x ≤ y`, and `k ∣ x` for `x y` in `Nat` or `Int`
(and `k` a literal), along with negations of these statements.

We decompose the sides of the inequalities as linear combinations of atoms.

If we encounter `x / k` or `x % k` for literal integers `k` we introduce new auxiliary variables
and the relevant inequalities.

On the first pass, we do not perform case splits on natural subtraction.
If `omega` fails, we recursively perform a case split on
a natural subtraction appearing in a hypothesis, and try again.

The options
omega +splitDisjunctions +splitNatSub +splitNatAbs +splitMinMax
can be used to:
* `splitDisjunctions`: split any disjunctions found in the context,
  if the problem is not otherwise solvable.
* `splitNatSub`: for each appearance of `((a - b : Nat) : Int)`, split on `a ≤ b` if necessary.
* `splitNatAbs`: for each appearance of `Int.natAbs a`, split on `0 ≤ a` if necessary.
* `splitMinMax`: for each occurrence of `min a b`, split on `min a b = a ∨ min a b = b`
Currently, all of these are on by default.","diagnostics":[]}

input.content:
# AlgebraicJacobian/Cohomology/BasicOpenCech.lean

## Outcome: PARTIAL — Step 1c LANDED inline (~40 LOC); Steps 2–4 deferred.

**Sorry count: 6 (unchanged); FILE COMPILES (`lean_diagnostic_messages` severity=error returns `[]`).**

Locations: L1120 (PAUSED), L1212, L1536, L1564, L1754, **L1846** (former L1802 `h_loc_exact`, shifted +44 by iter-109 inline Step 1c scaffolding).

## `h_loc_exact` at L1781 (sorry was L1802, now L1846)

### Step 1c — RESOLVED (closes "per-coord `IsLocalization.Away`" portion of the recipe)

Three inline `have`s landed at L1796–L1834 (preserving the iter-108 `h_V_le_U` + `h_slice_eq` Steps 1a+1b setup byte-for-byte at L1786–L1795):

1. **`h_pi_eq_inf'`** (L1799–L1812, 14 LOC): `∏ᶜ_a basicOpenCover ↑s₀ (x a) = (Finset.image x Finset.univ).inf' _ (basicOpenCover ↑s₀)`. Chain:
   - `∏ᶜ_a → ⨅ a` via `le_antisymm (le_iInf (Pi.π).le) (Pi.lift homOfLE).le`.
   - `⨅ a → Finset.univ.inf` via `Finset.inf_univ_eq_iInf`.
   - `Finset.univ.inf → Finset.univ.inf'` via `Finset.inf'_eq_inf`.
   - `Finset.univ.inf' (g ∘ x) → (Finset.image x univ).inf' g` via `Finset.inf'_image` (in reverse).

   This bridge unblocks the project's `basicOpenCover_finset_inf'_isAffineOpen` helper (which expects `Finset (↑s₀)`, not the cochain's native `Fin (n+1) → ↑s₀` indexing). The universe-mismatch on `CompleteLattice.finite_product_eq_finset_inf` (which requires `α, ι : Type u` same universe — `Fin (n+1) : Type 0` vs `Opens : Type u`) is sidestepped via `Pi.π`/`Pi.lift`-style `le_antisymm`.

2. **`h_V_affine`** (L1814–L1818, 5 LOC): `IsAffineOpen (∏ᶜ fun a => basicOpenCover ↑s₀ (x a))` via `h_pi_eq_inf' x ▸ basicOpenCover_finset_inf'_isAffineOpen hU ↑s₀ (Finset.image x Finset.univ) _`.

3. **`h_isLoc`** (L1822–L1834, 13 LOC): the per-coord `IsLocalization.Away ((presheafMap _).hom f.1) Γ(V_x ⊓ D(f.1))` via `(h_V_affine x).isLocalization_of_eq_basicOpen` + `h_slice_eq x`. Explicit `@`-form thread the algebra `((presheafMap inf_le_left).hom).toAlgebra` on the conclusion type (matching Mathlib's signature exactly, modeled on the project's `basicOpenCover_finset_inf'_isLocalization` at L330).

### Steps 2–4 — DEFERRED (per-x `IsScalarTower` + Pi-localization transport)

Trailing `sorry` at L1846 remains. Iter-109 prover ATTEMPTED Step 2 (algebra adapter via `instIsLocalizedModuleToLinearMapToAlgHomOfIsLocalizationAlgebraMapSubmonoid`) but hit a structural blocker:

**Step 2 attempts (lean_multi_attempt; not committed)**:

- **Attempt A: `letI` algebras + scTower in goal type**. Set up `alg1 : Algebra R Γ(V_x)`, `alg2 : Algebra Γ(V_x) Γ(V_x ⊓ D(f.1))`, `alg3 : Algebra R Γ(V_x ⊓ D(f.1))`, and `scTower : IsScalarTower` (via `IsScalarTower.of_algebraMap_eq` + `presheafMap_restrict_collapse`). The `letI` chain compiled as part of the goal type. **Blocker**: `letI` in the goal type does NOT propagate to the proof body's typeclass inference — the `infer_instance` fails to find the `Algebra` instances even though they appear in the type signature. Lean's `letI ... in <goal>` elaborates the `letI` immediately, so the body sees the goal without binders. Tried `intro alg1 alg2 alg3 scTower` — fails with "No additional binders to introduce".

- **Attempt B: explicit `@`-form on `IsLocalizedModule`**. Wrote the conclusion as `@IsLocalizedModule R _ (powers f.1) Γ(V_x) _ _ Γ(V_x ⊓ D(f.1)) _ <module-instance> <linear-map>`. **Blocker**: the explicit-argument order for `IsLocalizedModule` is brittle — argument 2 was misparsed as `Submonoid` instead of `CommSemiring R`, and `RingHom.toLinearMap` does not exist on bare `RingHom` (needs the algebra structure first). Would require ~60 more LOC of explicit-argument bookkeeping.

The clean path forward requires either:
- (i) Moving the algebra setup BEFORE the `have h_IsLocMod` so the instances are stable in the outer scope (but then they're not per-x; need to redefine V_x as a `set` block per x — but `set` inside a single `have` body doesn't easily express the per-x dependency).
- (ii) Building the per-x `IsLocalizedModule` as a term-mode construction via `IsLocalizedModule.mk` directly, avoiding the `instIsLocalizedModuleToLinearMapToAlgHomOfIsLocalizationAlgebraMapSubmonoid` instance entirely.
- (iii) Refactoring the architectural layer to make Steps 1c–4 a top-level helper lemma — explicitly disallowed iter-109 ("No new top-level helpers"), but the natural shape for iter-110+ if the C1 escape-valve fires.

### LOC counter

`h_loc_exact` body now spans L1783–L1846 = **64 LOC** (was L1783–L1802 = 20 LOC at iter-109 entry). Steps 1c additions = 40 LOC. Under the iter-107 strategy-critic soft cap of 150 LOC.

### Final `lean_diagnostic_messages` (severity=error)

```
{"result":{"success":true,"items":[],"failed_dependencies":[]}}
```

File compiles. No new axioms. No new top-level helpers. No new sorries (6 → 6).

## Recommendation for iter-110 plan agent

**This is the SECOND consecutive PARTIAL on L1846 (iter-108 PARTIAL on Steps 1a+1b; iter-109 PARTIAL closing Step 1c only).** Per the iter-107 strategy-critic exit criterion, the iter-110 plan should fire the **escape-valve menu** (per STRATEGY.md § "Phase A escape-valve menu"):

- **Option 1 (CHEAPEST, RECOMMENDED)**: defer L1846 as a named Mathlib-gap sorry. The structural blocker (Step 2 algebra-instance threading + Step 3 Pi-localization transport) is real and would take ~5–8 iters of dedicated work to close inline within the soft cap, OR ~150–250 LOC if architecturally refactored. Defer-as-gap preserves the iter-109 Step 1c progress as inert infrastructure (the per-coord `h_isLoc` is independent of L1846 closure and survives).
- **Option 2**: fire C1 promotion (LineBundle architectural refactor, 5–8 iters / 200–300 LOC). The USER_HINTS iter-107 "no approximation" framing favors this — but C1 is orthogonal to the L1846 obstruction, so firing C1 doesn't help L1846 specifically. Better as a parallel lane.
- **Option 3**: re-consult `mathlib-analogist` to design a "Step 2-only" closure pattern that avoids the `letI`-in-goal-type propagation issue. Concrete blocker is identified above (Attempts A+B); analogist should evaluate whether term-mode `IsLocalizedModule.mk` is feasible inline.

## Key Mathlib lemmas verified in scope (iter-109)

- ✓ `IsLocalizedModule.pi` (`Mathlib.RingTheory.TensorProduct.IsBaseChangePi`)
- ✓ `IsAffineOpen.isLocalization_of_eq_basicOpen` (`Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.AffineScheme`)
- ✓ `Function.Exact.iff_of_ladder_linearEquiv` (`Mathlib.Algebra.Exact`)
- ✓ `Function.Exact.of_ladder_linearEquiv_of_exact` (`Mathlib.Algebra.Exact`)
- ✓ `LinearEquiv.conj_exact_iff_exact` (`Mathlib.Algebra.Exact`) — fallback
- ✓ `IsLocalizedModule.iso` (`Mathlib.Algebra.Module.LocalizedModule.Basic`)
- ✓ `instIsLocalizedModuleToLinearMapToAlgHomOfIsLocalizationAlgebraMapSubmonoid` (`Mathlib.Algebra.Module.LocalizedModule.IsLocalization`)
- ✓ `Submonoid.map_powers` (`Mathlib.Algebra.Group.Submonoid.Membership`)

## iter-107 · parallel prover

The `letI ... in <goal-type>` form is a recurring trap for proof bodies that need
per-x typeclass instances: `letI` in the goal type elaborates eagerly during
type-checking, so the proof body sees the goal with `letI` already unfolded and
the instances are not actually in scope as instances. The body cannot recover
them via `intro`. This bit iter-109 hard when trying to thread per-x algebra
structures (alg1/alg2/alg3 + IsScalarTower) needed for Mathlib's
`instIsLocalizedModuleToLinearMapToAlgHomOfIsLocalizationAlgebraMapSubmonoid`
instance. A small lean4 skill reference page on "per-iter typeclass threading
patterns" (term-mode `IsLocalizedModule.mk` vs `letI` in body vs sub-`have`
pattern) would have saved significant iter time. The standard workaround —
`letI` in the body, not in the type — works but is non-obvious from the error
("introN failed: no binders").
EOF
